macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Cools to 3.5% in June, Easing Fed Rate Hike Fears
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index fell 0.4% in June, bringing annual inflation to 3.5% and core inflation to 2.6%, both below forecasts; markets now price an 83.4% chance the Fed holds rates steady in July.

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Vows to End 5-Year Inflation Surge After CPI Report
items.summary: In his first congressional testimony since confirmation, Fed Chair Kevin Warsh pledged to restore price stability as cooler inflation data provides room to delay further rate hikes despite rising Middle East tensions.

items.title: Dow Edges Up, S&P 500 and Nasdaq Rise on Cool Inflation and Strong Bank Earnings
items.summary: The Dow rose 0.05% to 52,526, S&P 500 gained 0.38% to 7,543.89, and Nasdaq surged 0.90% to 26,107.01, driven by solid results from Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Bank of America.

items.title: IBM Shares Plummet 25% After Disappointing Q2 Revenue Warning
items.summary: IBM stock tumbled nearly 25% after warning second-quarter revenue would fall below estimates, becoming the largest drag on the Dow despite broader market gains.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Above $80 as Trump Reinstates 'Iranian Blockade'
items.summary: Crude oil climbed more than $1.86 to $80.05 per barrel after President Trump announced plans to reinstate a maritime blockade targeting Iran-linked vessels, escalating U.S.-Iran tensions.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Surges 9% on Beat Q2 Profit Amid Dealmaking Pick-up
items.summary: Goldman Sachs jumped 9% after surpassing Q2 profit expectations, driven by increased dealmaking activity and geopolitical uncertainties boosting its trading business.

items.title: U.S. Debt Interest Hits $24 Billion Weekly as National Debt Climbs to $39.4 Trillion
items.summary: The Congressional Budget Office reports U.S. debt interest payments now reach $24 billion per week as the national debt surpasses $39.4 trillion, making financing costs one of the fastest-growing budget items.

items.title: Trump Proposes 20% Cargo Fee for Strait of Hormuz Maritime Protection
items.summary: President Trump stated the U.S. will assume long-term security control of the Strait of Hormuz and charge a 20% fee on commercial shipping, dramatically expanding Washington's role in the energy corridor.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Over $101M in Crypto Shorts Liquidated Following Cooler Inflation Data
items.summary: Markets surged after U.S. inflation fell to 3.5% (below 3.8% expectations), triggering over $101 million in short liquidations in one hour, with Ethereum leading at $56 million.

items.title: Crypto Investment Funds Record $281.8M Net Inflows, Ending 8-Week Outflow Streak
items.summary: Institutional demand recovered with Bitcoin receiving $197.4M and Ethereum $84.4M in net inflows last week, marking the first positive week since May.

items.title: Polymarket Refunds Users After Hackers Steal Crypto via Compromised Vendor
items.summary: Polymarket is refunding affected users after a third-party vendor was compromised, injecting malicious scripts into its frontend for some users.

items.title: UK Unveils Tokenized Finance Roadmap Projecting £33B Annual Economic Boost
items.summary: The UK government laid out a comprehensive roadmap for tokenized finance, forecasting an annual £33 billion boost to the economy.

items.title: Senate Agriculture Committee Advances Clarity Act Crypto Bill to Floor Vote
items.summary: The Senate Agriculture Committee voted 12-11 along party lines to advance its portion of the Clarity Act, a key crypto market structure bill, toward a full Senate floor vote.

items.title: Japan's Largest Security Token Platform Moves Nearly $3B to Avalanche
items.summary: Japan's leading security token platform transferred nearly $3 billion in assets to the Avalanche blockchain, signaling major institutional adoption.

items.title: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 Markets Surge to Nearly 50% of Perp Volume Amid Onchain Stock Trading Growth
items.summary: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 markets now account for nearly half of perpetual trading volume, driven by rapid growth in onchain stock trading.

items.title: Block Launches New Crypto Wallet with OLED Touchscreen Display
items.summary: Jack Dorsey's Block released a follow-up to Bitkey, featuring an OLED touchscreen for transaction verification and security authorization.

items.title: Fidelity Launches Own Stablecoin 'FIDD' on Platform and Crypto Exchanges
items.summary: Fidelity is launching its proprietary stablecoin, FIDD, available on its platform and major crypto exchanges.

items.title: Perpetuals.com Ends Acquisition Talks with Trump-Linked World Liberty Financial
items.summary: Global fintech company Perpetuals.com discontinued acquisition discussions with World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture backed by the Trump family.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Stolen Trade Secrets for AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing trade secrets to develop its upcoming, unrevealed AI hardware gadgets, marking a major legal clash between the iPhone maker and the ChatGPT developer.

items.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Models
items.summary: The U.S. government has lifted a weekslong ban on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions and allowed a limited release of its powerful Mythos AI model to select companies after addressing cybersecurity concerns.

items.title: White House Requests OpenAI Limit GPT-5.6 Sol Release
items.summary: The White House has requested OpenAI restrict the release of its new GPT-5.6 Sol model to a small number of government-approved partners due to the model's advanced capabilities and cybersecurity risks.

items.title: Meta Announces $9.1 Billion AI Data Center Investment in Canada
items.summary: Meta plans to invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first AI data center in Canada, which will be its largest facility outside the United States.

items.title: Reflection Secures $1 Billion Computing Deal with Nebius
items.summary: AI startup Reflection signed a deal exceeding $1 billion to secure computing capacity from Nebius, including access to Nvidia's latest chips, to support its AI operations.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Data Center Moratorium
items.summary: New York Governor will block new large data centers fueling AI for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the country's first statewide moratorium on such infrastructure.

items.title: IBM Stock Rout Signals AI Infrastructure Spending Shift
items.summary: IBM sparked a market rout after forecasting Q2 revenue below estimates, signaling that businesses are prioritizing spending on data-center infrastructure over software, highlighting AI's toll on the software sector.

items.title: South Korea Plans $518 Billion Chipmaking Hub for AI Demand
items.summary: South Korean tech giants are set to build a massive $518 billion chipmaking hub specifically designed to serve the soaring global demand for artificial intelligence hardware.

items.title: Germany Regulates Google AI Overviews and Perplexity Under Media Laws
items.summary: Germany's media regulator declared that Google's AI Overviews and Perplexity AI are subject to national media laws, increasing scrutiny on AI-generated content following a court ruling on Google's liability.

items.title: SoftBank CEO Claims $5 Trillion Annual AI Investment Needed by 2040
items.summary: SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son stated that AI development will require $5 trillion in annual global investment by 2040, dismissing concerns about an investment bubble as 'absurd'.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Microsoft eliminates 4,800 jobs, with Xbox gaming unit among hardest hit
items.summary: Microsoft announced Monday it is cutting 2.1% of its workforce (4,800 jobs), with the Xbox gaming division significantly impacted as part of broader restructuring.

items.title: Square Enix confirms Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis will end service on October 6
items.summary: Square Enix announced that the mobile and Steam spinoff Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis will reach end-of-service status on October 6, 2026.

items.title: Square Enix teases Kingdom Hearts 4 at Nintendo Direct with no release date
items.summary: Square Enix teased Kingdom Hearts 4 during a Nintendo Direct, though no release date has been announced for the RPG franchise sequel.

items.title: SGDQ 2026 speedrun raises $2.4M for Doctors Without Borders
items.summary: The Summer Games Done Quick 2026 speedrunning event successfully raised $2.4 million for Doctors Without Borders, marking a major charitable milestone.

items.title: PS5 emulator for Windows nears release with Demon Souls and GTA 5 boot success
items.summary: Four PS5 emulators are in development, with Sharp Emu booting Demon Souls remake and Kiteai PS5 successfully running GTA 5 menus and Powerwash Simulator on Windows.

items.title: Sony faces 305,000+ signature petition to keep physical PlayStation discs amid piracy surge
items.summary: A viral change.org petition titled "Don't Kill the Disc" has gathered over 300,000 signatures urging Sony to retain physical PlayStation games as piracy concerns rise.

items.title: Nintendo announces major change to Switch 2 digital game pricing
items.summary: Nintendo revealed a pricing adjustment for Switch 2 digital games that will allow some owners to save money on future purchases.

items.title: Animal Crossing: New Horizons 3.0 update released early with first new content in years
items.summary: The surprise early release of Animal Crossing: New Horizons 3.0 brings the first new content to the game for players on Switch and Switch 2 after years.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Hikes MacBook and iPad Prices Due to Memory Chip Shortage Driven by AI Demand
items.summary: Apple increased prices on popular MacBooks and iPads as rising costs for memory and storage chips—sparked by the AI boom—create a shortage affecting gadget availability and pricing across the industry.

items.title: Meta Invests $9.1 Billion for First AI Data Center in Canada, Largest Outside U.S.
items.summary: Meta announced a plan to invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first artificial intelligence data center in Canada, marking its largest data center project outside the United States.

items.title: IBM Shares Plunge 25% After Revenue Forecast Signals AI Infrastructure Spending Over Software
items.summary: IBM stock dropped sharply after the company forecast second-quarter revenue below estimates, revealing that businesses are prioritizing spending on data-center infrastructure over software due to AI demands.

items.title: Meta Withdraws AI Image Generation Feature Days After Launch Following Privacy Backlash
items.summary: Meta scrapped its new Muse AI image generation feature after privacy advocates and creators criticized it for allowing users to generate images using public Instagram photos without explicit consent.

items.title: U.S. Launches AI and Cybersecurity Coordination Group to Share Vulnerability Data
items.summary: The White House announced a formal group bringing together AI developers and essential service providers to share cybersecurity vulnerability information identified by advanced AI systems and coordinate responses.

items.title: TYLSemi Raises $43 Million to Build Custom AI Chip Components
items.summary: Startup TYLSemi, founded by executives from Qualcomm's recent AlphaWave acquisition, secured $43 million in early funding to help companies develop their own custom AI chips.

items.title: U.S. Authorities Unseal Charges Against Three Russians Linked to Ransomware Infrastructure
items.summary: U.S. officials unsealed charges and offered a $10 million reward for information on three Russians accused of providing computer infrastructure for ransomware attacks against U.S. critical infrastructure.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Moratorium on Large AI Data Centers
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers fueling artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the country's first statewide moratorium on such facilities.
